messages.author: hpux735
messages.text: This is from the MeshTNC tool I've been working on. These are live packets over the radio that are being dissected and decrypted (when it can find a key that works). It's using the brand new (and very rough) MeshCore crate https://crates.io/crates/MeshCore

messages.author: Pete
messages.text: Hi! I'm not sure if this library aims to be a tool for interacting using the companion protocol but I also have started writing a rust library for exactly that. I can start a separate thread if that's preferred.
messages.content_html: Hi! I'm not sure if this library aims to be a tool for interacting using the companion protocol but I also have started writing a rust library for exactly that. I can start a separate thread if that's preferred.

messages.author: hpux735
messages.text: Nah, this channel is fine. It was started for a packet parsing/writing library. As far as I know no one has done a companion library (edited)
messages.content_html: Nah, this channel is fine. It was started for a packet parsing/writing library. As far as I know no one has done a companion library (edited)

messages.author: hpux735
messages.text: This is where I'm developing at the moment. I think it would be really cool to have a companion library (USB and Bluetooth? One or the other?) to link to https://git.housedillon.com/wdillon/Meshcore-rs/src/branch/wdillon/non-owned
messages.content_html: This is where I'm developing at the moment. I think it would be really cool to have a companion library (USB and Bluetooth? One or the other?) to link to https://git.housedillon.com/wdillon/Meshcore-rs/src/branch/wdillon/non-owned
messages.reply_html:
Pete
Hi! I'm not sure if this library aims to be a tool for interacting using the companion protocol but I also have started writing a rust library for exactly that. I can start a separate thread if that's preferred.

messages.author: Pete
messages.text: Luckily it seems that the companion protocol is the same whether it's serial or bluetooth, so hopefully it's just a different hardware processing loop. Currently I'm only implementing the serial port version but I'm not averse to also doing the bluetooth side. I'm only concerned that although Rust's serial libraries are multiplatform, the bluetooth ones may not be, so that might be a sticking point. My work is in https://github.com/PeterGrace/meshcore_companion_rs -- definitely not feature complete from a companion protocol perspective but it's now functional to the point where I can both send and receive channel messages. I am the author of meshtui ( https://github.com/PeterGrace/meshtui) which is a tui-based client for Meshtastic, and one goal will be to replicate meshtui's functionality, more or less, for MeshCore, which is what has prompted the writing of this library.

messages.author: Pete
messages.text: I had originally started with bincode and its derivitives to handle serde like functionality but in the end it wasn't behaving itself and so I'm just doing it by hand with read_exact/read_to_end buffer pops

messages.author: Pete
messages.text: I wonder if your library might help with my library in that regard? I assume you're doing very similar constructions.
messages.content_html: I wonder if your library might help with my library in that regard? I assume you're doing very similar constructions.

messages.author: hpux735
messages.text: Yah. In mine I ended up making a work-alike to the Bytes type. It does a lot of work to be type safe, but it's very similar to what you're doing in a lot of ways. https://git.housedillon.com/wdillon/Meshcore-rs/src/branch/wdillon/non-owned/packet-lib/src/fixed_bytes.rs
messages.content_html: Yah. In mine I ended up making a work-alike to the Bytes type. It does a lot of work to be type safe, but it's very similar to what you're doing in a lot of ways. https://git.housedillon.com/wdillon/Meshcore-rs/src/branch/wdillon/non-owned/packet-lib/src/fixed_bytes.rs
